items.title: Steelmaker SSAB’s Earnings Rise Despite Higher Costs From Iran War
items.desc: Higher prices and shipments helped boost second-quarter earnings. High-strength steel markets in the Middle East were hurt by hostilities but the overall global market showed relatively stable demand.

items.title: UK Inflation Fell More Than Forecast in June on Cheaper Petrol
items.desc: Cheaper motor fuel helped bring down UK inflation to the lowest in over a year, delivering some relief for households as new Prime Minister Andy Burnham promises to tackle the cost of living.

items.title: DHS issues $84B in fines to illegal immigrants under Trump administration
items.desc: DHS has issued 103,000 civil fines totaling roughly $84 billion to illegal immigrants since Trump took office, collecting $1.2 billion in payments.

items.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries
items.desc: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

items.title: UN chief to head to Syria, first such visit since 2009
items.desc: U.N. chief Antonio Guterres will travel to Syria later this week to show his support for the political transition in the country -- the first visit by a U.N. secretary-general since 2009, the world body said on July 22.
